findings.title: Generic tool name can shadow trusted tools
findings.detail: Tool "search" uses a generic high-value name. When multiple servers load together, this can intercept calls meant for a trusted server.

evidence.snippet: search

findings.title: Description issues instructions to the model
findings.detail: Description of "dns_check" contains imperative instructions directed at the AI assistant rather than describing the tool. This is the core tool-poisoning technique.

evidence.snippet: Fast DNS-based domain existence check. Tests if a name is taken across many TLDs at once (faster than search, no pricing). Returns 'taken' (definitely registere…

findings.remediation: Treat the server as untrusted; the model may obey embedded instructions.

name: create_backorder
description: Place a backorder on a domain that is currently registered to someone else. We watch it and automatically register it for the user when it becomes available (drops) - availability is polled every few minutes. The user is charged only if the catch succeeds - no upfront fee. Requires a card on file or

name: list_backorders
description: List the user's backorders (watching, caught, failed, cancelled, expired). Optionally filter by status.

name: cancel_backorder
description: Stop watching a domain by cancelling a backorder. Only backorders in the 'watching' state can be cancelled.

name: list_webhooks
description: List all webhook endpoints configured for the user's account. Returns URL, subscribed events, and active status for each webhook.

name: create_webhook
description: Register a new webhook endpoint to receive event notifications. The URL must use HTTPS. Choose which events to subscribe to (e.g. domain.purchased, transfer.completed). The webhook secret is returned only once - save it to verify incoming payloads with HMAC-SHA256.

name: update_webhook
description: Update an existing webhook's URL, subscribed events, or active status. Use list_webhooks to get the webhook ID first.

name: delete_webhook
description: Delete a webhook endpoint. All pending deliveries will be cancelled. This action cannot be undone.

name: list_webhook_deliveries
description: Get recent delivery attempts for a webhook - shows event type, HTTP status, number of attempts, and any errors. Useful for debugging webhook issues.

name: setup_domain_email
description: Pre-configure email DNS on a domain (optional). This is called automatically by create_mailbox when needed. Use this only to pre-configure DNS before creating mailboxes. Pass force=true to override existing email provider (Google Workspace, Fastmail, Proton).

name: get_domain_email_status
description: Check if email is enabled on a domain and whether DNS records are verified. Returns record status and mailbox count.

name: create_mailbox
description: Create an email address. Specify slug (required) and optionally domain. For custom domains: email DNS is auto-configured on first use. For imported domains (external registrar): returns DNS records to add manually at your registrar. For domani.run: omit domain. Max 5 mailboxes per account, 1 free @d

name: list_mailboxes
description: List all your email addresses. Optionally filter by domain. API: GET /api/emails (or GET /api/email).

name: check_email_deliverability
description: Run the same deliverability and abuse-safety checks used by send_email, without sending or consuming quota. Use this before authentication, payment, onboarding, or high-volume messages. API: POST /api/emails/{address}/deliverability-check.

name: send_email
description: Send an email from one of your mailboxes. Specify domain and slug to identify the sender mailbox. Supports text and/or HTML body, CC/BCC, attachments (base64), and threading via In-Reply-To/References. Rate limited to 100 sends per hour per mailbox. API: POST /api/emails/{address}/send.

name: list_email_messages
description: List messages in a system folder or virtual view. Defaults to Inbox. Supports pagination, direction and search filters. API: GET /api/emails/{address}/messages.

name: list_email_folders
description: List canonical mailbox folders, virtual views, server-derived total/unread counts, and backend capabilities. API: GET /api/emails/{address}/folders.

name: update_email_messages
description: Apply a reversible mailbox lifecycle action to up to 100 messages, or permanently delete messages already in Trash with email:delete. API: POST /api/emails/{address}/messages/actions.

name: get_message
description: Get a single message by ID with full content, headers, delivery events, and attachment download URLs. API: GET /api/emails/{address}/messages/{id}.

name: set_email_webhook
description: Set or remove the inbound webhook URL for a mailbox. When set, incoming emails are POSTed to this URL in real-time with HMAC-SHA256 signing. Must be HTTPS. Set to null to remove. API: PUT /api/emails/{address}/webhook or DELETE /api/emails/{address}/webhook.

name: rotate_email_webhook_secret
description: Regenerate the HMAC signing secret for a mailbox webhook. The old secret is immediately invalidated. API: POST /api/emails/{address}/webhook/rotate.

name: test_email_webhook
description: Send a signed test payload to the mailbox webhook URL. Returns HTTP status and success/failure. API: POST /api/emails/{address}/webhook/test.

name: set_email_forward
description: Configure inbound handling for a mailbox: forward incoming emails to another address (in addition to storing) and/or drop spam-flagged messages. Set forward_to to null to remove forwarding. API: PATCH /api/emails/{address}.

name: add_mailbox_alias
description: Add an alias address that delivers into an existing mailbox, without using a mailbox slot. Useful for multiple public addresses (sales@, hello@, contact@) landing in one inbox. The alias must be on the same domain as the mailbox. API: POST /api/emails/{address}/aliases.

name: list_mailbox_aliases
description: List all alias addresses that deliver into a mailbox. API: GET /api/emails/{address}/aliases.

name: remove_mailbox_alias
description: Remove an alias address from a mailbox. Email to that address stops being delivered. API: DELETE /api/emails/{address}/aliases/{alias}.

name: set_catch_all
description: Route any email sent to an unmatched address on a domain into a designated mailbox. Anything that does not match a mailbox, subaddress, or alias lands in the catch-all. The mailbox must already exist on the domain. API: PUT /api/domains/{domain}/email/catch-all.

name: clear_catch_all
description: Remove the catch-all on a domain. Email to unmatched addresses will be dropped again. API: DELETE /api/domains/{domain}/email/catch-all.

name: list_suppressions
description: List addresses on your suppression list - hard bounces and complaints (added automatically) plus manual entries. Sends to these addresses are skipped. API: GET /api/suppressions.

name: add_suppression
description: Manually add an address to your suppression list so future sends skip it. Hard bounces and complaints are added automatically - use this for addresses you want to stop emailing. API: POST /api/suppressions.

name: remove_suppression
description: Remove an address from your suppression list so you can email it again (e.g. after the recipient fixed their mailbox). API: DELETE /api/suppressions/{address}.

name: add_mail_rule
description: Add an inbound filtering rule to a mailbox. If a message matches (by from/to/subject/body), the action runs: drop, mark_read, forward (to action_arg), webhook_only (skip forward-to), or label (with action_arg). Rules run in priority order (lower first); first match wins. API: POST /api/emails/{addre

name: list_mail_rules
description: List a mailbox's inbound filtering rules, in the priority order they are applied. API: GET /api/emails/{address}/rules.

name: remove_mail_rule
description: Remove an inbound filtering rule from a mailbox. API: DELETE /api/emails/{address}/rules/{ruleId}.

name: delete_mailbox
description: Delete a mailbox and all its messages (including attachments). Requires confirm=true. Call without confirm first to see what will be deleted. API: DELETE /api/emails/{address}.

name: mark_messages_read
description: Mark one or more messages as read or unread. Useful for tracking which messages have been processed by your agent. API: PATCH /api/emails/{address}/messages/read.

name: delete_message
description: Move an email message to Trash. This is reversible with update_email_messages action=restore. API: DELETE /api/emails/{address}/messages/{id}.

name: delete_messages
description: Move multiple messages to Trash. This is reversible with update_email_messages action=restore. API: POST /api/emails/{address}/messages/delete.

name: forward_message
description: Forward an email message to another address. Includes the original message context (sender, date, subject, body). Optionally prepend a note. Subject is auto-prefixed with 'Fwd:'. API: POST /api/emails/{address}/messages/{id}/forward.

name: reply_to_message
description: Reply to an email message. Automatically sets To (from Reply-To or sender), Subject (Re: prefix), In-Reply-To and References headers for proper threading. Use all=true for reply-all (auto-populates CC from original recipients, excluding your own address). API: POST /api/emails/{address}/messages/{id

name: check_email
description: Check email DNS health for a domain: MX records, SPF, DMARC, DKIM. Auto-detects the email provider from MX records.

name: get_email_deliverability
description: Get an owner-scoped email health report for a domain. Separates deterministic DNS readiness, 30-day bounce and complaint outcomes, account safety state, and measured inbox placement. A readiness score is not an Inbox probability. API: GET /api/domains/{domain}/email/deliverability.

name: create_hosted_mailbox
description: Create a real IMAP/SMTP mailbox on a domain you own (connect Apple Mail, Thunderbird, or any mail client), instead of an API mailbox. Provisions the mailbox on our mail server, publishes DNS when we manage it, and returns the DNS records, mail client settings, and a one-time app password. Requires a

name: get_mailbox_client_settings
description: Get IMAP/SMTP settings for a hosted mailbox to configure a mail client (Apple Mail, Thunderbird). Username is the full address; password is an app password. API: GET /api/emails/{address}/client-settings.

name: create_mailbox_credential
description: Create an app password for a hosted mailbox, used as the password in a mail client. The secret is returned once - store it. API: POST /api/emails/{address}/credentials.

name: list_mailbox_credentials
description: List the app passwords for a hosted mailbox (labels + metadata; secrets are shown only once at creation). API: GET /api/emails/{address}/credentials.

name: revoke_mailbox_credential
description: Revoke an app password on a hosted mailbox. Any mail client using it stops connecting. API: DELETE /api/emails/{address}/credentials/{id}.

findings.title: Lethal trifecta: untrusted input + private data + external sink
findings.detail: Server "mcp" exposes tools that together enable an exfiltration flow. Untrusted content: get_nameservers, setup_billing, upgrade_plan, create_token, list_audit_events, domain_status, watch_transfer, list_providers, list_webhooks, create_webhook, update_webhook, list_webhook_deliveries, get_domain_email_status, list_mailboxes, check_email_deliverability, list_email_messages, list_email_folders, get_message, list_mailbox_aliases, add_suppression, remove_suppression, list_mail_rules, mark_messages_read, check_email, get_email_deliverability, get_mailbox_client_settings, list_mailbox_credentials · Private data: get_account, buy_domain, get_dns, set_dns, snapshot_dns, restore_dns, clone_dns, get_dnssec, add_dnssec, delete_dnssec, list_tokens, create_token, list_audit_events, revoke_token, connect_domain, verify_connection, verify_service, whois_lookup, set_whois_privacy, set_contact, resend_email_verification, import_domain, verify_import, set_parking, set_listing_price, respond_to_broker_inquiry, get_broker_inquiry_state, create_backorder, create_webhook, get_domain_email_status, create_mailbox, rotate_email_webhook_secret, add_mailbox_alias, check_email, create_hosted_mailbox, get_mailbox_client_settings, create_mailbox_credential, list_mailbox_credentials, revoke_mailbox_credential · Public sink: buy_domain, provision_agent, set_dns, claim_identity, update_identity, add_dnssec, get_nameservers, upgrade_plan, create_token, connect_domain, domain_status, set_domain_redirect, resend_email_verification, watch_transfer, list_providers, respond_to_broker_inquiry, list_webhooks, create_webhook, update_webhook, delete_webhook, list_webhook_deliveries, setup_domain_email, get_domain_email_status, create_mailbox, list_mailboxes, check_email_deliverability, send_email, list_email_messages, list_email_folders, update_email_messages, set_email_webhook, rotate_email_webhook_secret, test_email_webhook, set_email_forward, add_mailbox_alias, remove_mailbox_alias, set_catch_all, clear_catch_all, add_suppression, remove_suppression, add_mail_rule, delete_message, delete_messages, forward_message, reply_to_message, check_email, get_email_deliverability, create_hosted_mailbox, create_mailbox_credential · Destructive: claim_identity, list_identities, delete_dnssec, create_token, import_domain, set_listing_price, unsell_domain, create_backorder, delete_webhook, create_mailbox, check_email_deliverability, update_email_messages, set_email_webhook, set_email_forward, remove_mailbox_alias, clear_catch_all, remove_suppression, add_mail_rule, remove_mail_rule, delete_mailbox, delete_message, delete_messages, revoke_mailbox_credential.

findings.remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.

remediation: Do not co-install these capabilities under one trust boundary; an injected comment/email can steer the model to exfiltrate via the sink tool.
